text.title: Query Enhancement Patterns
text.summary: Query expansion (multi-phrasing), HyDE (hypothetical document embedding), and query rewrite for better RAG retrieval recall.

rule.statement: Use query expansion to generate 3 alternative phrasings when the original query may not match the corpus vocabulary; include the original query in the search set.
rule.rationale: User queries often use different vocabulary than the documents. Multi-phrasing retrieval averages over the vocabulary gap and increases recall.

example.code:
from openai import OpenAI client = OpenAI()
def expand_query(query: str) -> list[str]: resp = client.chat.completions.create( model="gpt-4o-mini", messages=[ {"role": "system", "content": "Generate 3 alternative phrasings of the question. Return only the questions, one per line."}, {"role": "user", "content": query}, ], temperature=0.7, ) variations = resp.choices[0].message.content.strip().split("\n") return [query] + [v.strip() for v in variations if v.strip()]

rule.statement: Account for 2x embedding cost per query when enabling HyDE; disable for cost-sensitive or low-QPS production workloads.
rule.rationale: HyDE generates a hypothetical answer first, then embeds it instead of (or alongside) the raw query. This doubles embedding API calls per request.

example.code: def generate_hypothetical_answer(query: str) -> str: """HyDE: embed the hypothetical answer instead of the raw query.""" resp = client.chat.completions.create( model="gpt-4o-mini", messages=[ {"role": "system", "content": "Answer the question as if you had access to relevant documents. Be specific."}, {"role": "user", "content": query}, ], temperature=0.5, ) return resp.choices[0].message.content
